name: Param_AnnotationUnitsSettings
summary: (No description provided in vendor docs for Grasshopper.Rhinoceros.Annotations.Params.Param_AnnotationUnitsSettings.)

constructors.name: Param_AnnotationUnitsSettings

methods.name: AddedToDocument
methods.summary: This method will be called when an object is added to a document. Override this method if you want to handle such events.

params.name: document
params.doc: Document that now owns this object.

methods.name: AddRuntimeMessage
methods.signature: public virtual void AddRuntimeMessage(GH_RuntimeMessageLevel level, string text)
methods.summary: Add a new message to this object. Valid message type flags are Warning and Error. If the Message string is empty or zero-length no message is added.

params.name: level
params.doc: Type of message. Only Warnings and Errors are recorded.

methods.name: AddSource(IGH_Param)
methods.summary: Append a new Source parameter to the end of the Sources list. Sources provide this parameter with data at runtime.

methods.name: AddSource(IGH_Param, Int32)
methods.signature: public virtual void AddSource(IGH_Param source, int index)
methods.summary: Insert a new Source parameter into the Sources list. Sources provide this parameter with data at runtime.

methods.name: AddVolatileData(GH_Path, Int32, T)
methods.signature: public bool AddVolatileData(GH_Path path, int index, T data)
methods.summary: (Inherited from GH_Param<T>.)

methods.name: AddVolatileData(GH_Path, Int32, Object)
methods.signature: public bool AddVolatileData(GH_Path path, int index, Object data)
methods.summary: Inserts an item of volatile data into the data structure.

params.name: path
params.doc: The branch path of the data. If the path doesn't exist yet, it will be created.

params.name: index
params.doc: The item index of the data. If the path doesn't contain the index yet, it will be enlarged to encompass the index.

returns.doc: True on success, False on failure. If the data cannot be converted, the topology will remain unmolested.

methods.name: AppendAdditionalMenuItems
methods.summary: Override this function if you want to add specific items to the object context menu Default items already included in the menu toolstrip are: 1. Name 2. Preview 3. Warnings 4. Errors

methods.name: AppendMenuItems
methods.summary: This function is called when a context menu is about to be displayed. Override it to set custom items. GH_ActiveObject will already populate the menu with default items, if you merely wish to insert object-specific menu item, consider overriding AppendAdditionalMenuItems instead.

returns.doc: If true, the menu will be displayed, if false the menu will be supressed.

methods.name: DocumentContextChanged
methods.signature: public virtual void DocumentContextChanged(GH_Document document, GH_DocumentContext context)
methods.summary: This method will be called when the document that owns this object moves into a different context.

methods.name: ExpirePreview
methods.summary: Call this function when you suspect that the preview has expired for this object. This will cause the display cache to be eradicated.

methods.name: ExpireSolution
methods.summary: Informs the document that owns this object that the solution has expired. The current object will be set to BLANK as a result. This method is recursive, it will also expire any and all objects which depend on this object. If you want a less destructive expiration, consider using ClearData(). If this object is already Blank, you should consider not expiring it.

params.name: recompute
params.doc: If True, the document will be instructed to solve the new state.

methods.name: ExpireSolutionTopLevel
methods.summary: Invoke the Expiresolution(bool) method on the toplevel object.

params.name: recompute
params.doc: If true, a new computation will start right away.

methods.name: IsolateObject
methods.summary: (Inherited from GH_Param<T>.)

methods.name: MovedBetweenDocuments
methods.signature: public virtual void MovedBetweenDocuments(GH_Document oldDocument, GH_Document newDocument)
methods.summary: This method will be called when an object is moved from one document to another. Override this method if you want to handle such events.

params.name: oldDocument
params.doc: Document that used to own this object.

params.name: newDocument
params.doc: Document that now owns ths object.

methods.name: NewInstanceGuid()
methods.summary: Generate a new random instance GUID

methods.name: NewInstanceGuid(Guid)
methods.summary: Set the instance ID to be a specific GUID. This is very dangerous, only use this function if you're 6"4' and your first name is David.

methods.name: OnPingDocument
methods.summary: Raise the PingDocument Event on the toplevel object and try to find the document which owns this object.

returns.doc: The document which owns this object if successful, or nothing if no document owns this object.

methods.name: OnPreviewExpired
methods.summary: Raises the PreviewExpired event on the toplevel object.

params.name: redraw
params.doc: If True, the viewports will be immediately redrawn.

methods.name: OnSolutionExpired
methods.summary: Raises the SolutionExpired event on the toplevel object. You probably want to call ExpireSolution() instead of this method directly.

params.name: recompute
params.doc: If True, the solution will be immediately recalculated.

methods.name: Read
methods.summary: (Inherited from GH_Param<T>.)

methods.name: ReadFull
methods.summary: GH_InstanceDescription does not by default serialize all fields. Use this function to read all fields from the archive. This method is compatible with the default Write()/Read() operations.

returns.doc: True on success, false on failure.

methods.name: RecordUndoEvent(GH_UndoRecord)
methods.summary: Record an entire undo record.

methods.name: RecordUndoEvent(String)
methods.summary: Record a generic object change undo event.

returns.doc: The ID of the newly added record or Guid.Empty on failure.

methods.signature: public Guid RecordUndoEvent(string undoName, IGH_UndoAction action)
methods.summary: Record a specific object change undo event.

methods.name: RegisterRemoteIDs
methods.summary: Override this function if you want object changes in the Rhino document to trigger a new solution.

params.name: id_list
params.doc: The object ID database Grasshopper is responsive to.

methods.name: RelinkProxySources
methods.summary: Attempt to replace all proxy sources with real sources. Proxy sources are used during file IO, when actual sources might not be available yet. Once an IO operation has been completed there should be no more proxy sources.

params.name: document
params.doc: The document from which to harvest the real source parameters.

returns.doc: True on success, false on failure.

methods.name: RemoveAllSources
methods.summary: Remove all sources from this parameter.

methods.name: RemovedFromDocument
methods.summary: This method will be called when an object is removed from a document. Override this method if you want to handle such events.

params.name: document
params.doc: Document that now no longer owns this object.

methods.name: RemoveEffects
methods.summary: Remove all post-process effects. Note to implementors, you must call the base method if you override this function.

methods.name: RemoveSource(Guid)
methods.summary: Remove the specified source from this parameter.

methods.name: RemoveSource(IGH_Param)
methods.summary: Remove the specified source from this parameter.

methods.name: ReplaceSource(Guid, IGH_Param)
methods.signature: public virtual void ReplaceSource(Guid old_source_id, IGH_Param new_source)
methods.summary: Replace an existing source with a new one. If the old_source does not exist in this parameter, nothing happens.

methods.name: ReplaceSource(IGH_Param, IGH_Param)
methods.signature: public virtual void ReplaceSource(IGH_Param old_source, IGH_Param new_source)
methods.summary: Replace an existing source with a new one. If the old_source does not exist in this parameter, nothing happens.

methods.name: RuntimeMessages
methods.summary: Gets the list of cached runtime messages that were recorded during solver-time processes.

returns.doc: A list of runtime message strings.

methods.signature: public virtual bool SDKCompliancy(int exeVersion, int exeServiceRelease)
methods.summary: Test whether this object is compliant with a given Rhino version.

returns.doc: True if this object is compliant with the given Rhino release.

methods.name: SetIconOverride
methods.summary: Set a new custom icon override for this object.

params.name: customIcon
params.doc: Icon override. Should be a 24x24 image.

methods.signature: public bool SetPrincipal(bool set, bool recordUndo, bool recompute)
methods.summary: Set the principal parameter state.

methods.name: TriggerAutoSave()
methods.summary: Triggers the AutoSave function on the owner document with the object_changed flag.

methods.name: TriggerAutoSave(GH_AutoSaveTrigger)
methods.summary: Triggers the AutoSave function on the owner document with a custom flag.

params.name: trigger
params.doc: Reason for the autosave operation. It is possible that a user has decided to avoid autosave events for specific types, so if you can, try and provide a correct trigger flag.

methods.name: TriggerAutoSave(Guid)
methods.summary: Triggers the AutoSave function on the owner document with the object_changed flag.

params.name: id
params.doc: ID of autosave event. Consecutive autosave requests with the same ID will be ignored.

methods.name: TriggerAutoSave(GH_AutoSaveTrigger, Guid)
methods.signature: public void TriggerAutoSave(GH_AutoSaveTrigger trigger, Guid id)
methods.summary: Triggers the AutoSave function on the owner document with a custom flag.

params.name: trigger
params.doc: Reason for the autosave operation. It is possible that a user has decided to avoid autosave events for specific types, so if you can, try and provide a correct trigger flag.

params.name: id
params.doc: ID of autosave event. Consecutive autosave requests with the same ID will be ignored.

methods.name: Write
methods.summary: (Inherited from GH_Param<T>.)

methods.name: WriteFull
methods.summary: GH_InstanceDescription does not by default serialize all fields. Use this function to write all fields to the archive. This method is compatible with the default Write()/Read() operations.

returns.doc: True on success, false on failure.

properties.name: Access
properties.summary: Gets or sets the Access level for this parameter.

properties.name: Attributes
properties.summary: Gets or sets the attributes that are associated with this object. Only set custom attributes if you know what you are doing.

properties.name: Category
properties.summary: Gets or sets the Category in which this object belongs. If HasCategory() returns false, this field has no meaning.

properties.name: ComponentGuid
properties.summary: (Overrides GH_DocumentObject.ComponentGuid.)

properties.name: DataMapping
properties.summary: Gets or sets the data mapping of this Parameter.

properties.name: DataType
properties.summary: Type is either Void if there are no sources or Remote. Derived classes should expand on this.

properties.name: Description
properties.summary: Gets or sets the description of the object. This field typically remains fixed during the lifetime of an object.

properties.name: Exposure
properties.summary: (Overrides GH_DocumentObject.Exposure.)

properties.name: HasCategory
properties.summary: Gets whether or not the Category field has been set.

properties.name: HasProxySources
properties.summary: Gets a value indicating whether or not this parameter maintains proxy sources. Proxy sources are used during file IO, when actual sources might not be available yet. Once an IO operation has been completed there should be no more proxy sources.

properties.name: HasSubCategory
properties.summary: Gets whether or not the SubCategory field has been set.

properties.name: Icon_24x24
properties.summary: The icon associated with this object.

properties.name: Icon_24x24_Locked
properties.summary: The greyscale icon of this object.

properties.name: IconCapableUI
properties.summary: By default the NickName menu item supports the Icon Mode override toggle. If your UI is not capable of displaying icons, then override this property and return False.

properties.name: IconDisplayMode
properties.summary: Gets the current display mode of the object.

properties.name: InstanceDescription
properties.summary: Gets the description of this instance. The default description is about the amount and source of the local values.

properties.name: InstanceGuid
properties.summary: Gets the ID of this runtime instance.

properties.name: IsDataProvider
properties.summary: (Inherited from GH_Param<T>.)

properties.name: IsPrincipal
properties.summary: Gets whether this parameter is a principal parameter. Principal parameters are maintained by components and are not part of the IGH_Param interface.

properties.name: Keywords
properties.summary: Gets a list of additional keywords that describe the object. Typically this list is empty but you can override this property to aid in object searches.

properties.name: Kind
properties.summary: Gets the parameter kind. The kind is evaluated lazily and cached.

properties.name: Locked
properties.summary: Gets or sets the enabled flag of this object. Disabled objects are ignored during solutions.

properties.name: MutableNickName
properties.summary: Gets or sets a value that enables Nick name changes through the menu. The default is TRUE.

properties.name: Name
properties.summary: Gets or sets the name of the object. This field typically remains fixed during the lifetime of an object.

properties.name: NickName
properties.summary: Gets or sets the nickname of the object. This field can be changed by the user.

properties.name: Obsolete
properties.summary: Gets whether this object is obsolete. Default implementation returns true if the class name contains the string "OBSOLETE" or if this class has been decorated with the ObsoleteAttribute. You are free to override this if you want, but I suggest adding the ObsoleteAttribute instead.

properties.name: Optional
properties.summary: Gets or sets whether or not this parameter is considered optional by the owner component. Empty, non-optional parameters prevent the component from being solved.

properties.name: Phase
properties.summary: Gets or sets the solution phase this object is currenly in.

properties.name: ProcessorTime
properties.summary: (Inherited from GH_Param<T>.)

properties.name: ProxySourceCount
properties.summary: Gets the number of proxy sources for this parameter. Proxy sources are used during file IO, when actual sources might not be available yet. Once an IO operation has been completed there should be no more proxy sources.

properties.name: Recipients
properties.summary: Gets a list of all the recipients of this parameter. I.e. a recipient has this parameter as one of the sources. The Recipient list is maintained by the parameter, do not modify it yourself.

properties.name: Reverse
properties.summary: Gets or sets the data reverse modifier of this parameter.

properties.name: RuntimeMessageLevel
properties.summary: Returns the worst case flag for the current object If the object contains at least 1 error, the result is Error.If the object contains at least 1 warning, the result is Warning.If the object contains at least 1 message, the result is Remark.If the object contains no errors, no warnings and no messages, the result is Blank.

properties.name: Simplify
properties.summary: Gets or sets the simplify modifier for this parameter.

properties.name: SourceCount
properties.summary: Gets the number of sources for this parameter.

properties.name: Sources
properties.summary: Gets a list of source parameters. Do not modify this list, if you wish to add or remove sources, use dedicated functions like AddSource() and RemoveSource() instead.

properties.name: StateTags
properties.summary: Gets all the StateTags that are associated with this parameter. A state tag is a visual feedback icon that represents specific internal settings.

properties.name: SubCategory
properties.summary: Gets or sets the SubCategory in which this object belongs. If HasSubCategory() returns false, this field has no meaning.

properties.name: Type
properties.summary: Gets the Framework Type descriptor for the stored Data.

properties.name: TypeName
properties.summary: (Inherited from ModelDataParam<T>.)

properties.name: VolatileData
properties.summary: (Inherited from GH_Param<T>.)

properties.name: VolatileDataCount
properties.summary: (Inherited from GH_Param<T>.)

properties.name: WireDisplay
properties.summary: Gets or sets the wire display style for this parameter. Wire display only affects the wires connected to the parameter input.

events.name: AttributesChanged
events.summary: Raised whenever the number or kind of attributes changes. This event is handled by GH_Documents who subsequently wipe their attribute caches.

events.name: DisplayExpired
events.summary: Raised whenever the display (on the Canvas) of a certain object becomes invalid.

events.name: PingDocument
events.summary: Raised whenever an object needs to know which GH_Document it belongs to.

events.name: PreviewExpired
events.summary: Raised whenever the display (in the Rhino viewports) of a certain object becomes invalid.

events.name: SolutionExpired
events.summary: Raised whenever the solution of a certain object becomes invalid.
